perturbation.original_text: The Disclosee agree s that the Seller s or Advisors shall not have any liability to the Disclosee or to any of their directors, employees, advisors or agents resulting from the use of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Disclosee agrees that the Sellers or Advisors shall not have any liability to the Disclosee or to any of their directors, employees, advisors or agents.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'resulting from the use of Confidential Information,' the clause now suggests no liability for any reason, which contradicts the document's core purpose of protecting confidential information. If the Disclosee suffers damages for reasons unrelated to confidential information, this section may be interpreted as limiting liability for such events and creating an in-text contradiction, even though the agreement is focussed on confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Sellers agreeing to disclose confidential information (the “ Confidential Information ”) to the Candidate (“Disclosee ”), the latter hereby undertakes to the Sellers that as Disclosee , they will keep confidential all and any Confidential Information received at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the 'no liability' clause to issues 'resulting from the use of Confidential Information.' The changed text removes this limitation, creating a blanket 'no liability' statement. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which explicitly states that the Disclosee undertakes to keep confidential all Confidential Information. The changed text could be interpreted as preventing the Sellers from holding the Disclosee liable even if the Disclosee breaches confidentiality, which is the core purpose of the agreement and what the Contradicted Text is intended to enforce. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language was removed creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall in no way constitute a commitment by any person to supply any Confidential Inform ation or enter into any contract in connection with the Sellers .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall in no way constitute a commitment by any person to enter into any contract in connection with the Sellers .
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'to supply any Confidential Information' creates an internal contradiction. The entire agreement is predicated on the exchange of confidential information, but this modified sentence now implies there is no commitment to actually provide such information. This contradicts the purpose clause at the beginning of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Sellers agreeing to disclose confidential information (the “ Confidential Information ”) to the Candidate (“Disclosee ”), the latter hereby undertakes to the Sellers that as Disclosee , they will keep confidential all and any Confidential Information received at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement does not constitute a commitment to supply confidential information, but the changed text removes this clause. The contradicted text states that the agreement exists "In consideration of the Sellers agreeing to disclose confidential information", which directly conflicts with the changed text's implication that there is no commitment to provide such information. The removal of 'to supply any Confidential Information' creates an in-text contradiction by implying there is no obligation to provide confidential information, while the contradicted text states that the agreement exists because the sellers *are* disclosing confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Disclosee or any of its Representatives becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information to a regulatory authority or to any other entity or third party, the Disclosee shall immediately notify the Sellers before disclosing such Confidential In formation, so that the Sellers may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, without prejudice to t he Disclosee’s remedies under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Disclosee or any of its Representatives becomes legally compelled to disclose any information to a regulatory authority or to any other entity or third party, the Disclosee shall immediately notify the Sellers before disclosing such information, so that the Sellers may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, without prejudice to t he Disclosee’s remedies under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'of the Confidential Information' and replacing it with 'any information', the scope of mandatory notification expands significantly. This now requires notification even for information not considered confidential under the original agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction as it broadens the notification requirement beyond the defined scope of 'Confidential Information', thus affecting the whole agreement when it defines Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information shall mean the following: a) all such information pertaining to the Sale of the Munt and the Sellers as may be provided to the Disclosee and their Representatives, by the Sellers, their advisors or their representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the notification requirement to 'Confidential Information'. The changed text broadens this to 'any information'. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' narrowly. Therefore, the changed text requires notification even for information not defined as confidential, directly contradicting the scope defined in the 'Confidential Information' section. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because the Disclosee is now obligated to notify the Sellers about disclosing a much wider range of information than initially agreed upon, which would affect the enforceability of the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: It is further understood and agreed that, without prejudice to any other rights or remedies which either party may have, any brea ch of these undertakings could cause the Sellers and/or its shareholders irreparable injury.
perturbation.changed_text: It is further understood and agreed that, without prejudice to any other rights or remedies which either party may have.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'any breach of these undertakings could cause the Sellers and/or its shareholders irreparable injury' eliminates an acknowledgement of potential damages. This is contradictory because the agreement's enforcement mechanisms (injunctive relief, specific performance) imply that breaches *will* cause harm, creating an internal contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of an actual or threatened breach by the Disclosee and/or its Representatives of any provisions of this Agreement , the Sellers shall be entitled (i) t o seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction restraining the Disclosee and/or its Representatives from breaching the terms hereof or from disclosing any Confidential Information to any person and (ii) specific performance and other equit able relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text acknowledges potential irreparable injury to the Sellers and/or its shareholders if there is a breach. The changed text removes this acknowledgement. However, the contradicted text specifies that in the event of a breach, the Sellers are entitled to seek injunctive relief and specific performance, implying that a breach would cause harm. The removal of the acknowledgement of harm directly contradicts the section that specifies remedies for breach. Thus, the change introduces an in-text contradiction because the agreement's enforcement mechanisms (injunctive relief, specific performance) imply that breaches will cause harm, creating an internal contradiction, as stated in the perturbation explanation.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of an actual or threatened breach by the Disclosee and/or its Representatives of any provisions of this Agreement , the Sellers shall be entitled (i) t o seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction restraining the Disclosee and/or its Representatives from breaching the terms hereof or from disclosing any Confidential Information to any person and (ii) specific performance and other equit able relief.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of an actual or threatened breach by the Disclosee and/or its Representatives of any provisions of this Agreement , the Sellers shall be entitled (ii) specific performance and other equit able relief.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the right to seek injunctive relief creates a significant in-text contradiction. The specific performance clause is only relevant if an actual harm from a disclosure has occurred; the injunction would prevent the harm in the first place. Eliminating injunctive relief significantly weakens the agreement's protective power, contradicting its stated purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Sellers agreeing to disclose confidential information (the “ Confidential Information ”) to the Candidate (“Disclosee ”), the latter hereby undertakes to the Sellers that as Disclosee , they will keep confidential all and any Confidential Information received at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides for injunctive relief and specific performance. The changed text removes the right to seek injunctive relief. The contradicted text states that the Disclosee will keep confidential all Confidential Information received. The original text provides the Sellers with the means of stopping a harmful disclosure with injunctive relief. Removing injunctive relief weakens the agreement's protective power, directly contradicting the Disclosee's undertaking to keep the information confidential as now the Sellers cannot prevent a disclosure.
